POST /accounts/{account_id}/workers/obse.description: Run a temporary or saved query.

chart.description: When true, includes time-series data in the response.

compare.description: When true, includes a comparison dataset from the previous time period of equal length.

dry.description: When true, executes the query without persisting the results. Useful for validation or previewing.

granularity.description: Number of time-series buckets. Only used when view is 'calculations'. Omit to let the system auto-detect an appropriate granularity.

ignoreSeries.description: When true, omits time-series data from the response and returns only aggregated values. Reduces response size when series are not needed.

limit.description: Maximum number of events to return when view is 'events'. Also controls the number of group-by rows when view is 'calculations'.

offset.description: Cursor for pagination in event, trace, and invocation views. Pass the $metadata.id of the last returned item to fetch the next page.

offsetBy.description: Numeric offset for paginating grouped/pattern results (top-N lists). Use together with limit. Not used by cursor-based pagination.

offsetDirection.description: Pagination direction: 'next' for forward, 'prev' for backward.

parameters.description: Query parameters defining what data to retrieve — filters, calculations, group-bys, and ordering. In practice this should always be provided for ad-hoc queries. Only omit when executing a previously saved query by queryId. Use the keys and values endpoints to discover available fields before building filters.

calculations.description: Aggregation calculations to compute (e.g. count, avg, p99). Each calculation produces aggregate values and optional time-series data.

alias.description: Custom label for this calculation in the results. Useful for distinguishing multiple calculations.

key.description: Field name to calculate over. Must exist in the data — verify with the keys endpoint. Omit for operators that don't require a key (e.g. count).

keyType.description: Data type of the key. Required when key is provided to ensure correct aggregation.

operator.description: Aggregation operator to apply. Examples: count, avg, sum, min, max, median, p90, p95, p99, uniq, stddev, variance.

datasets.description: Datasets to query. Leave empty to query all available datasets.

filterCombination.description: Logical operator for combining top-level filters: 'and' (all must match) or 'or' (any must match). Defaults to 'and'.

filters.description: Filters to narrow query results. Use the keys and values endpoints to discover available fields before building filters. Supports nested groups via kind: 'group'. Maximum nesting depth is 4.

anyOf.description: A filter condition applied to query results. Use the keys and values endpoints to discover available fields and their values before constructing filters.

key.description: Filter field name. Use verified keys from previous query results or the keys endpoint. Common keys include $metadata.service, $metadata.origin, $metadata.trigger, $metadata.message, and $metadata.error.

kind.description: Discriminator for leaf filter nodes. Always 'filter' when present; may be omitted.

operation.description: Comparison operator. String operators: includes, not_includes, starts_with, ends_with, regex. Existence: exists, is_null. Set membership: in, not_in (comma-separated values). Numeric: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte.

type.description: Data type of the filter field. Must match the actual type of the key being filtered.

value.description: Comparison value. Must match actual values in your data — verify with the values endpoint. Ensure the value type (string/number/boolean) matches the field type. String comparisons are case-sensitive. Regex uses RE2 syntax (no lookaheads/lookbehinds).

anyOf.description: A group node that nests child filters combined with a logical operator.

filterCombination.description: Logical operator for combining child filters: 'and' (all must match) or 'or' (any must match).

filters.description: Child filter nodes. Each can be a leaf filter or another nested group.

anyOf.description: A filter condition applied to query results. Use the keys and values endpoints to discover available fields and their values before constructing filters.

key.description: Filter field name. Use verified keys from previous query results or the keys endpoint. Common keys include $metadata.service, $metadata.origin, $metadata.trigger, $metadata.message, and $metadata.error.

kind.description: Discriminator for leaf filter nodes. Always 'filter' when present; may be omitted.

operation.description: Comparison operator. String operators: includes, not_includes, starts_with, ends_with, regex. Existence: exists, is_null. Set membership: in, not_in (comma-separated values). Numeric: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte.

type.description: Data type of the filter field. Must match the actual type of the key being filtered.

value.description: Comparison value. Must match actual values in your data — verify with the values endpoint. Ensure the value type (string/number/boolean) matches the field type. String comparisons are case-sensitive. Regex uses RE2 syntax (no lookaheads/lookbehinds).

kind.description: Discriminator indicating this is a nested filter group.

anyOf.description: A filter condition applied to query results. Use the keys and values endpoints to discover available fields and their values before constructing filters.

key.description: Filter field name. Use verified keys from previous query results or the keys endpoint. Common keys include $metadata.service, $metadata.origin, $metadata.trigger, $metadata.message, and $metadata.error.

kind.description: Discriminator for leaf filter nodes. Always 'filter' when present; may be omitted.

operation.description: Comparison operator. String operators: includes, not_includes, starts_with, ends_with, regex. Existence: exists, is_null. Set membership: in, not_in (comma-separated values). Numeric: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte.

type.description: Data type of the filter field. Must match the actual type of the key being filtered.

value.description: Comparison value. Must match actual values in your data — verify with the values endpoint. Ensure the value type (string/number/boolean) matches the field type. String comparisons are case-sensitive. Regex uses RE2 syntax (no lookaheads/lookbehinds).

allOf.description: Supports nested groups via kind: 'group'. Maximum nesting depth is 4.

groupBys.description: Fields to group calculation results by. Only applicable when the query view is 'calculations'. Produces per-group aggregate values.

type.description: Data type of the group-by field.

value.description: Field name to group results by (e.g. $metadata.service, $metadata.statusCode).

havings.description: Post-aggregation filters applied to calculation results. Use to filter groups after aggregation (e.g. only groups where count > 100).

key.description: Calculation alias or operator to filter on after aggregation.

operation.description: Numeric comparison operator: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte.

value.description: Threshold value to compare the calculation result against.

limit.description: Maximum number of group-by rows to return in calculation results. A value of 10 is a sensible default for most use cases.

needle.description: Full-text search expression applied across all event fields. Matches events containing the specified text.

isRegex.description: When true, treats the value as a regular expression (RE2 syntax).

matchCase.description: When true, performs a case-sensitive search. Defaults to case-insensitive.

value.description: The text or pattern to search for.

orderBy.description: Ordering for grouped calculation results. Only effective when a group-by is present.

order.description: Sort direction: 'asc' for ascending, 'desc' for descending.

value.description: Alias of the calculation to order results by. Must match the alias (or operator) of a calculation in the query.

queryId.description: Identifier for the query. When parameters are omitted, this ID is used to load a previously saved query's parameters. When providing parameters inline, pass any identifier (e.g. an ad-hoc ID).

timeframe.description: Timeframe for the query using Unix timestamps in milliseconds. Narrower timeframes produce faster responses and more specific results.

from.description: Start timestamp for the query timeframe (Unix timestamp in milliseconds)

to.description: End timestamp for the query timeframe (Unix timestamp in milliseconds)

view.description: Controls the shape of the response. 'events': individual log lines matching the query. 'calculations': aggregated metrics (count, avg, p99, etc.) with optional group-by breakdowns and time-series. 'invocations': events grouped by request ID. 'traces': distributed trace summaries. 'agents': Durable Object agent summaries.

requestBody.description: Query your observability events, requests, and traces to build visualizations and identify insights.

result.description: Complete results of a query run. The populated fields depend on the requested view type (events, calculations, invocations, traces, or agents).

agents.description: Durable Object agent summaries. Present when the query view is 'agents'. Each entry represents an agent with its event counts and status.

agentClass.description: Class name of the Durable Object agent.

eventTypeCounts.description: Breakdown of event counts by event type.

firstEventMs.description: Timestamp of the earliest event from this agent in the queried window (Unix epoch ms).

hasErrors.description: Whether the agent emitted any error events in the queried window.

lastEventMs.description: Timestamp of the most recent event from this agent (Unix epoch ms).

namespace.description: Durable Object namespace the agent belongs to.

service.description: Worker service name that hosts this agent.

totalEvents.description: Total number of events emitted by this agent in the queried window.

calculations.description: Aggregated calculation results. Present when the query view is 'calculations'. Contains computed metrics (count, avg, p99, etc.) with optional group-by breakdowns and time-series data.

compare.description: Comparison calculation results from the previous time period. Present when the compare option is enabled. Same structure as calculations.

events.description: Individual event results. Present when the query view is 'events'. Contains the matching log lines and their metadata.

count.description: Total number of events matching the query (may exceed the number returned due to limits).

events.description: List of individual telemetry events matching the query.

items.description: A single telemetry event representing a log line, span, or metric data point emitted by a Worker.

$containers.description: Cloudflare Containers event information that enriches your logs for identifying and debugging issues.

$metadata.description: Structured metadata extracted from the event. These fields are indexed and available for filtering and aggregation.

cloudService.description: Cloudflare product that generated this event (e.g. workers, pages).

cost.description: Estimated cost units for this invocation.

endTime.description: Span end time as a Unix epoch in milliseconds.

error.description: Error message, present when the log represents an error.

errorTemplate.description: Templatized version of the error message used for grouping similar errors.

fingerprint.description: Content-based fingerprint used to group similar events.

id.description: Unique event ID. Use as the cursor value for offset-based pagination.

level.description: Log level (e.g. log, debug, info, warn, error).

messageTemplate.description: Templatized version of the log message used for grouping similar messages.

metricName.description: Metric name when the event represents a metric data point.

origin.description: Origin of the event (e.g. fetch, scheduled, queue).

parentSpanId.description: Span ID of the parent span in the trace hierarchy.

region.description: Cloudflare data center / region that handled the request.

requestId.description: Cloudflare request ID that ties all logs from a single invocation together.

service.description: Worker script name that produced this event.

spanId.description: Span ID for this individual unit of work within a trace.

startTime.description: Span start time as a Unix epoch in milliseconds.

statusCode.description: HTTP response status code returned by the Worker.

traceDuration.description: Total duration of the entire trace in milliseconds.

traceId.description: Distributed trace ID linking spans across services.

transactionName.description: Logical transaction name for this request.

trigger.description: What triggered the invocation (e.g. GET /users, POST /orders, queue message).

url.description: Request URL that triggered the Worker invocation.

$workers.description: Cloudflare Workers event information that enriches your logs for identifying and debugging issues.

dataset.description: The dataset this event belongs to (e.g. cloudflare-workers).

source.description: Raw log payload. May be a string or a structured object depending on how the log was emitted.

timestamp.description: Event timestamp as a Unix epoch in milliseconds.

fields.description: List of fields discovered in the matched events. Useful for building dynamic UIs.

key.description: Field name present in the matched events.

type.description: Data type of the field (string, number, or boolean).

series.description: Time-series data for the matched events, bucketed by the query granularity.

items.description: A single telemetry event representing a log line, span, or metric data point emitted by a Worker.

$containers.description: Cloudflare Containers event information that enriches your logs for identifying and debugging issues.

$metadata.description: Structured metadata extracted from the event. These fields are indexed and available for filtering and aggregation.

cloudService.description: Cloudflare product that generated this event (e.g. workers, pages).

cost.description: Estimated cost units for this invocation.

endTime.description: Span end time as a Unix epoch in milliseconds.

error.description: Error message, present when the log represents an error.

errorTemplate.description: Templatized version of the error message used for grouping similar errors.

fingerprint.description: Content-based fingerprint used to group similar events.

id.description: Unique event ID. Use as the cursor value for offset-based pagination.

level.description: Log level (e.g. log, debug, info, warn, error).

messageTemplate.description: Templatized version of the log message used for grouping similar messages.

metricName.description: Metric name when the event represents a metric data point.

origin.description: Origin of the event (e.g. fetch, scheduled, queue).

parentSpanId.description: Span ID of the parent span in the trace hierarchy.

region.description: Cloudflare data center / region that handled the request.

requestId.description: Cloudflare request ID that ties all logs from a single invocation together.

service.description: Worker script name that produced this event.

spanId.description: Span ID for this individual unit of work within a trace.

startTime.description: Span start time as a Unix epoch in milliseconds.

statusCode.description: HTTP response status code returned by the Worker.

traceDuration.description: Total duration of the entire trace in milliseconds.

traceId.description: Distributed trace ID linking spans across services.

transactionName.description: Logical transaction name for this request.

trigger.description: What triggered the invocation (e.g. GET /users, POST /orders, queue message).

url.description: Request URL that triggered the Worker invocation.

$workers.description: Cloudflare Workers event information that enriches your logs for identifying and debugging issues.

dataset.description: The dataset this event belongs to (e.g. cloudflare-workers).

source.description: Raw log payload. May be a string or a structured object depending on how the log was emitted.

timestamp.description: Event timestamp as a Unix epoch in milliseconds.

invocations.description: Events grouped by invocation (request ID). Present when the query view is 'invocations'. Each key is a request ID mapping to all events from that invocation.

run.description: Represents a single execution of a query against Workers Observability data, including the query definition, execution status, and performance statistics.

accountId.description: Cloudflare account ID that owns this query run.

created.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the query run was created.

dry.description: Whether this was a dry run (results not persisted).

granularity.description: Number of time-series buckets used for the query. Higher values produce more detailed series data.

id.description: Unique identifier for this query run.

adhoc.description: If the query wasn't explcitly saved

calculations.description: Create Calculations to compute as part of the query.

datasets.description: Set the Datasets to query. Leave it empty to query all the datasets.

filterCombination.description: Set a Flag to describe how to combine the filters on the query.

filters.description: Configure the Filters to apply to the query. Supports nested groups via kind: 'group'.

anyOf.description: A filter condition applied to query results. Use the keys and values endpoints to discover available fields and their values before constructing filters.

key.description: Filter field name. Use verified keys from previous query results or the keys endpoint. Common keys include $metadata.service, $metadata.origin, $metadata.trigger, $metadata.message, and $metadata.error.

kind.description: Discriminator for leaf filter nodes. Always 'filter' when present; may be omitted.

operation.description: Comparison operator. String operators: includes, not_includes, starts_with, ends_with, regex. Existence: exists, is_null. Set membership: in, not_in (comma-separated values). Numeric: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte.

type.description: Data type of the filter field. Must match the actual type of the key being filtered.

value.description: Comparison value. Must match actual values in your data — verify with the values endpoint. Ensure the value type (string/number/boolean) matches the field type. String comparisons are case-sensitive. Regex uses RE2 syntax (no lookaheads/lookbehinds).

groupBys.description: Define how to group the results of the query.

havings.description: Configure the Having clauses that filter on calculations in the query result.

limit.description: Set a limit on the number of results / records returned by the query

needle.description: Define an expression to search using full-text search.

orderBy.description: Configure the order of the results returned by the query.

order.description: Set the order of the results

value.description: Configure which Calculation to order the results by.

query.description: A saved query definition with its parameters, metadata, and ownership information.

statistics.description: Query performance statistics from the database (does not include network latency).

abr_level.description: The level of Adaptive Bit Rate (ABR) sampling used for the query. If empty the ABR level is 1

bytes_read.description: Number of uncompressed bytes read from the table.

elapsed.description: Time in seconds for the query to run.

rows_read.description: Number of rows scanned from the table.

status.description: Current execution status of the query run.

timeframe.description: Time range for the query execution

from.description: Start timestamp for the query timeframe (Unix timestamp in milliseconds)

to.description: End timestamp for the query timeframe (Unix timestamp in milliseconds)

updated.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the query run was last updated.

userId.description: ID of the user who initiated the query run.

statistics.description: Query performance statistics from the database. Includes execution time, rows scanned, and bytes read. Does not include network latency.

abr_level.description: The level of Adaptive Bit Rate (ABR) sampling used for the query. If empty the ABR level is 1

bytes_read.description: Number of uncompressed bytes read from the table.

elapsed.description: Time in seconds for the query to run.

rows_read.description: Number of rows scanned from the table.

traces.description: Trace summaries matching the query. Present when the query view is 'traces'. Each entry represents a distributed trace with its spans, duration, and services involved.

errors.description: Error messages encountered during the trace, if any.

rootSpanName.description: Name of the root span that initiated the trace.

rootTransactionName.description: Logical transaction name for the root span.

service.description: List of Worker services involved in the trace.

spans.description: Total number of spans in the trace.

traceDurationMs.description: Total duration of the trace in milliseconds.

traceEndMs.description: Trace end time as a Unix epoch in milliseconds.

traceId.description: Unique identifier for the distributed trace.

traceStartMs.description: Trace start time as a Unix epoch in milliseconds.
